text.KJV: Love righteousness, ye that be judges of the earth: think of the Lord with a good (heart,) and in simplicity of heart seek him.
text.NABRE: I. The Reward of Righteousness Chapter 1 Exhortation to Righteousness, the Key to Life Love righteousness, you who judge the earth; think of the Lord in goodness, and seek him in integrity of heart;
text.NRSV: Exhortation to Uprightness 1 think of the Lord in goodness and seek him with sincerity of heart;
text.DRB: Love justice, you that judge the earth. Think of our Lord in goodness, and in simplicity of heart seek him:

text.KJV: For he will be found of them that tempt him not; and sheweth himself unto such as do not distrust him.
text.NABRE: Because he is found by those who do not test him, and manifests himself to those who do not disbelieve him.
text.NRSV: because he is found by those who do not put him to the test, and manifests himself to those who do not distrust him.
text.DRB: because he is found of them that tempt him not: and he appeareth to them that have faith in him.

text.KJV: For froward thoughts separate from God: and his power, when it is tried, reproveth the unwise.
text.NABRE: For perverse counsels separate people from God, and his power, put to the proof, rebukes the foolhardy;
text.NRSV: For perverse thoughts separate people from God, and when his power is tested, it exposes the foolish;
text.DRB: For perverse cogitations separate from God: and proved power chasteneth the unwise:

text.KJV: For into a malicious soul wisdom shall not enter; nor dwell in the body that is subject unto sin.
text.NABRE: Because into a soul that plots evil wisdom does not enter, nor does she dwell in a body under debt of sin.
text.NRSV: because wisdom will not enter a deceitful soul, or dwell in a body enslaved to sin.
text.DRB: because wisdom will not enter into a malicious soul, nor dwell in a body subject to sins.

text.KJV: For the holy spirit of discipline will flee deceit, and remove from thoughts that are without understanding, and will not abide when unrighteousness cometh in.
text.NABRE: For the holy spirit of discipline flees deceit and withdraws from senseless counsels and is rebuked when unrighteousness occurs.
text.NRSV: For a holy and disciplined spirit will flee from deceit, and will leave foolish thoughts behind, and will be ashamed at the approach of unrighteousness.
text.DRB: For the Holy Ghost of discipline will fly from him that feigneth, and will withdraw himself from the cogitations that are without understanding, and he shall be chastened of iniquity ensuing.

text.KJV: For wisdom is a loving spirit; and will not acquit a blasphemer of his words: for God is witness of his reins, and a true beholder of his heart, and a hearer of his tongue.
text.NABRE: For wisdom is a kindly spirit, yet she does not acquit blasphemous lips; Because God is the witness of the inmost self and the sure observer of the heart and the listener to the tongue.
text.NRSV: For wisdom is a kindly spirit, but will not free blasphemers from the guilt of their words; because God is witness of their inmost feelings, and a true observer of their hearts, and a hearer of their tongues.
text.DRB: For the spirit of wisdom is gentle, and will not deliver the curser from his lips: because God is witness of his reins, and he is a true searcher of his heart, and an hearer of his tongue.

text.KJV: For the Spirit of the Lord filleth the world: and that which containeth all things hath knowledge of the voice.
text.NABRE: For the spirit of the Lord fills the world, is all-embracing, and knows whatever is said.
text.NRSV: Because the spirit of the Lord has filled the world, and that which holds all things together knows what is said,
text.DRB: Because the Spirit of our Lord hath replenished the whole world: and that which containeth all things, hath the knowledge of voice.

text.KJV: Therefore he that speaketh unrighteous things cannot be hid: neither shall vengeance, when it punisheth, pass by him.
text.NABRE: Therefore those who utter wicked things will not go unnoticed, nor will chastising condemnation pass them by.
text.NRSV: therefore those who utter unrighteous things will not escape notice, and justice, when it punishes, will not pass them by.
text.DRB: For this cause he that speaketh unjust things, cannot be hid, neither shall the chastising judgement pass him.
For in the cogitations of the impious there shall be examination: and the hearing of his works shall come to God, to the chastising of his iniquities.
text.KJV: For inquisition shall be made into the counsels of the ungodly: and the sound of his words shall come unto the Lord for the manifestation of his wicked deeds.
text.NABRE: For the devices of the wicked shall be scrutinized, and the sound of their words shall reach the Lord, for the chastisement of their transgressions;
text.NRSV: For inquiry will be made into the counsels of the ungodly, and a report of their words will come to the Lord, to convict them of their lawless deeds;

text.DRB: Because the ear of jealousy heareth all things, and the tumult of murmurings shall not be hid.
text.KJV: For the ear of jealousy heareth all things: and the noise of murmurings is not hid.
text.NABRE: Because a jealous ear hearkens to everything, and discordant grumblings are not secret.
text.NRSV: because a jealous ear hears all things, and the sound of grumbling does not go unheard.

text.DRB: Keep yourselves therefore from murmuring, which profiteth nothing, and refrain your tongue from detraction, because an obscure speech shall not pass in vain: and the mouth that lieth, killeth the soul.
text.KJV: Therefore beware of murmuring, which is unprofitable; and refrain your tongue from backbiting: for there is no word so secret, that shall go for nought: and the mouth that belieth slayeth the soul.
text.NABRE: Therefore guard against profitless grumbling, and from calumny withhold your tongues; For a stealthy utterance will not go unpunished, and a lying mouth destroys the soul.
text.NRSV: Beware then of useless grumbling, and keep your tongue from slander; because no secret word is without result, and a lying mouth destroys the soul.

text.KJV: Seek not death in the error of your life: and pull not upon yourselves destruction with the works of your hands.
text.NABRE: Do not court death by your erring way of life, nor draw to yourselves destruction by the works of your hands.
text.NRSV: Do not invite death by the error of your life, or bring on destruction by the works of your hands;
text.DRB: Zeal not death in the error of your life, neither procure ye perdition by the works of your hands.

text.KJV: For God made not death: neither hath he pleasure in the destruction of the living.
text.NABRE: Because God did not make death, nor does he rejoice in the destruction of the living.
text.NRSV: because God did not make death, and he does not delight in the death of the living.
text.DRB: Because God made not death, neither doth he rejoice in the perdition of the living.

text.KJV: For he created all things, that they might have their being: and the generations of the world were healthful; and there is no poison of destruction in them, nor the kingdom of death upon the earth:
text.NABRE: For he fashioned all things that they might have being, and the creatures of the world are wholesome; There is not a destructive drug among them nor any domain of Hades on earth,
text.NRSV: For he created all things so that they might exist; the generative forces of the world are wholesome, and there is no destructive poison in them, and the dominion of Hades is not on earth.
text.DRB: For he created all things to be: and he made the nations of the earth to health: and there is no medicine of destruction in them, nor Kingdom of hell in the earth.

text.KJV: (For righteousness is immortal:)
text.NABRE: For righteousness is undying.
text.NRSV: For righteousness is immortal.
text.DRB: For justice is perpetual and immortal.

text.KJV: But ungodly men with their works and words called it to them: for when they thought to have it their friend, they consumed to nought, and made a covenant with it, because they are worthy to take part with it.
text.NABRE: The Wicked Reject Immortality and Righteousness Alike It was the wicked who with hands and words invited death, considered it a friend, and pined for it, and made a covenant with it, Because they deserve to be allied with it.
text.NRSV: Life as the Ungodly See It But the ungodly by their words and deeds summoned death; considering him a friend, they pined away and made a covenant with him, because they are fit to belong to his company.
text.DRB: But the impious with hands & words have provoked it: and esteeming it a friend, have fallen to decay, and have made covenants with it: because they are worthy to be of the part thereof.

text.KJV: For the ungodly said, reasoning with themselves, but not aright, Our life is short and tedious, and in the death of a man there is no remedy: neither was there any man known to have returned from the grave.
text.NABRE: Chapter 2 For, not thinking rightly, they said among themselves: “Brief and troubled is our lifetime; there is no remedy for our dying, nor is anyone known to have come back from Hades.
text.NRSV: 2 ‘Short and sorrowful is our life, and there is no remedy when a life comes to its end, and no one has been known to return from Hades.
text.DRB: For they have said thinking with themselves not well: Little, and with tediousness is the time of our life: and in the end of a man there is no recovery, and there is none known that hath returned from hell:

text.KJV: For we are born at all adventure: and we shall be hereafter as though we had never been: for the breath in our nostrils is as smoke, and a little spark in the moving of our heart:
text.NABRE: For by mere chance were we born, and hereafter we shall be as though we had not been; Because the breath in our nostrils is smoke, and reason a spark from the beating of our hearts,
text.NRSV: For we were born by mere chance, and hereafter we shall be as though we had never been, for the breath in our nostrils is smoke, and reason is a spark kindled by the beating of our hearts;
text.DRB: because of nothing were we born, and after this we shall be as if we had not been: because the breath is a smoke in our nostrils: & speech a spark to move our heart.

Which being extinguished, our body shall be ashes, and the spirit shall be poured abroad as soft air, and our life shall pass as the trace of a cloud, and shall be dissolved as a mist, which is driven away by the beams of the sun, and oppressed with the heat thereof:
text.KJV: Which being extinguished, our body shall be turned into ashes, and our spirit shall vanish as the soft air,
text.NABRE: And when this is quenched, our body will be ashes and our spirit will be poured abroad like empty air.
text.NRSV: when it is extinguished, the body will turn to ashes, and the spirit will dissolve like empty air.

text.DRB: and our name in time shall be forgotten, and no man shall have remembrance of our works.
text.KJV: And our name shall be forgotten in time, and no man shall have our works in remembrance, and our life shall pass away as the trace of a cloud, and shall be dispersed as a mist, that is driven away with the beams of the sun, and overcome with the heat thereof.
text.NABRE: Even our name will be forgotten in time, and no one will recall our deeds. So our life will pass away like the traces of a cloud, and will be dispersed like a mist Pursued by the sun’s rays and overpowered by its heat.
text.NRSV: Our name will be forgotten in time, and no one will remember our works; our life will pass away like the traces of a cloud, and be scattered like mist that is chased by the rays of the sun and overcome by its heat.

text.DRB: For our time is the passing of a shadow, and there is no return of our end: because it is sealed, and no man returneth.
text.KJV: For our time is a very shadow that passeth away; and after our end there is no returning: for it is fast sealed, so that no man cometh again.
text.NABRE: For our lifetime is the passing of a shadow; and our dying cannot be deferred because it is fixed with a seal; and no one returns.
text.NRSV: For our allotted time is the passing of a shadow, and there is no return from our death, because it is sealed up and no one turns back.

text.KJV: Come on therefore, let us enjoy the good things that are present: and let us speedily use the creatures like as in youth.
text.NABRE: Come, therefore, let us enjoy the good things that are here, and make use of creation with youthful zest.
text.NRSV: ‘Come, therefore, let us enjoy the good things that exist, and make use of the creation to the full as in youth.
text.DRB: Come therefore, and let us enjoy the good things that are, and let us quickly use the creature as in youth.
Let us fill ourselves with precious wine, and ointments: and let not the flower of the time pass us.
text.KJV: Let us fill ourselves with costly wine and ointments: and let no flower of the spring pass by us:
text.NABRE: Let us have our fill of costly wine and perfumes, and let no springtime blossom pass us by;
text.NRSV: Let us take our fill of costly wine and perfumes, and let no flower of spring pass us by.

text.DRB: Let us crown ourselves with roses, before they wither: let there be no meadow, which our riot shall not pass through.
text.KJV: Let us crown ourselves with rosebuds, before they be withered:
text.NABRE: let us crown ourselves with rosebuds before they wither.
text.NRSV: Let us crown ourselves with rosebuds before they wither.

text.DRB: Let none of us be exempted from our riotousness: every-where let us leave signs of joy: because this is our portion, and this our lot.
text.KJV: Let none of us go without his part of our voluptuousness: let us leave tokens of our joyfulness in every place: for this is our portion, and our lot is this.
text.NABRE: Let no meadow be free from our wantonness; everywhere let us leave tokens of our merriment, for this is our portion, and this our lot.
text.NRSV: Let none of us fail to share in our revelry; everywhere let us leave signs of enjoyment, because this is our portion, and this our lot.

text.DRB: Let us oppress the poor just man, and not spare the widow, nor reverence the old man's grey head of long time.
text.KJV: Let us oppress the poor righteous man, let us not spare the widow, nor reverence the ancient gray hairs of the aged.
text.NABRE: Let us oppress the righteous poor; let us neither spare the widow nor revere the aged for hair grown white with time.
text.NRSV: Let us oppress the righteous poor man; let us not spare the widow or regard the grey hairs of the aged.

text.DRB: But let our strength be the law of justice: for that which is weak, is found unprofitable.
text.KJV: Let our strength be the law of justice: for that which is feeble is found to be nothing worth.
text.NABRE: But let our strength be our norm of righteousness; for weakness proves itself useless.
text.NRSV: But let our might be our law of right, for what is weak proves itself to be useless.

text.KJV: Therefore let us lie in wait for the righteous; because he is not for our turn, and he is clean contrary to our doings: he upbraideth us with our offending the law, and objecteth to our infamy the transgressings of our education.
text.NABRE: Let us lie in wait for the righteous one, because he is annoying to us; he opposes our actions, Reproaches us for transgressions of the law and charges us with violations of our training.
text.NRSV: ‘Let us lie in wait for the righteous man, because he is inconvenient to us and opposes our actions; he reproaches us for sins against the law, and accuses us of sins against our training.
text.DRB: Let us therefore circumvent the just, because he is unprofitable to us, and he is contrary to our works, and reproachfully objecteth unto us the sins of the law, and defameth in us the sins of our discipline.

text.KJV: He professeth to have the knowledge of God: and he calleth himself the child of the Lord.
text.NABRE: He professes to have knowledge of God and styles himself a child of the Lord.
text.NRSV: He professes to have knowledge of God, and calls himself a child of the Lord.
text.DRB: He boasteth that he hath the knowledge of God, and nameth himself the son of God.
He is made unto us to the defaming of our cogitations.
text.KJV: He was made to reprove our thoughts.
text.NABRE: To us he is the censure of our thoughts; merely to see him is a hardship for us,
text.NRSV: He became to us a reproof of our thoughts;

text.DRB: He is grievous unto us even to behold, because his life is unlike to others, & his ways are changed.
text.KJV: He is grievous unto us even to behold: for his life is not like other men’s, his ways are of another fashion.
text.NABRE: Because his life is not like that of others, and different are his ways.
text.NRSV: the very sight of him is a burden to us, because his manner of life is unlike that of others, and his ways are strange.

text.DRB: We are esteemed of him as triflers, and he abstaineth from our ways as from uncleanness, and he preferreth the later ends of the just, and glorieth that he hath God for his father.
text.KJV: We are esteemed of him as counterfeits: he abstaineth from our ways as from filthiness: he pronounceth the end of the just to be blessed, and maketh his boast that God is his father.
text.NABRE: He judges us debased; he holds aloof from our paths as from things impure. He calls blest the destiny of the righteous and boasts that God is his Father.
text.NRSV: We are considered by him as something base, and he avoids our ways as unclean; he calls the last end of the righteous happy, and boasts that God is his father.

text.DRB: Let us see therefore if his words be true, and let us prove what things shall come to him, and we shall know what shall be his later end.
text.KJV: Let us see if his words be true: and let us prove what shall happen in the end of him.
text.NABRE: Let us see whether his words be true; let us find out what will happen to him in the end.
text.NRSV: Let us see if his words are true, and let us test what will happen at the end of his life;

text.DRB: For if he be the true son of God, he will defend him, & will deliver him from the hands of the adversaries.
text.KJV: For if the just man be the son of God, he will help him, and deliver him from the hand of his enemies.
text.NABRE: For if the righteous one is the son of God, God will help him and deliver him from the hand of his foes.
text.NRSV: for if the righteous man is God’s child, he will help him, and will deliver him from the hand of his adversaries.

text.DRB: By contumely and torment let us examine him, that we may know his reverence, and prove his patience.
text.KJV: Let us examine him with despitefulness and torture, that we may know his meekness, and prove his patience.
text.NABRE: With violence and torture let us put him to the test that we may have proof of his gentleness and try his patience.
text.NRSV: Let us test him with insult and torture, so that we may find out how gentle he is, and make trial of his forbearance.

text.DRB: To a most shameful death let us condemn him: for there shall be respect had unto him by his words.
text.KJV: Let us condemn him with a shameful death: for by his own saying he shall be respected.
text.NABRE: Let us condemn him to a shameful death; for according to his own words, God will take care of him.”
text.NRSV: Let us condemn him to a shameful death, for, according to what he says, he will be protected.’

text.KJV: Such things they did imagine, and were deceived: for their own wickedness hath blinded them.
text.NABRE: These were their thoughts, but they erred; for their wickedness blinded them,
text.NRSV: Error of the Wicked Thus they reasoned, but they were led astray, for their wickedness blinded them,
text.DRB: These things have they thought, and have erred: for their malice hath blinded them.

text.KJV: As for the mysteries of God, they knew them not: neither hoped they for the wages of righteousness, nor discerned a reward for blameless souls.
text.NABRE: And they did not know the hidden counsels of God; neither did they count on a recompense for holiness nor discern the innocent souls’ reward.
text.NRSV: and they did not know the secret purposes of God, nor hoped for the wages of holiness, nor discerned the prize for blameless souls;
text.DRB: And they have not known the sacraments of God, nor hoped for the reward of justice, nor esteemed the honour of holy souls.

text.KJV: For God created man to be immortal, and made him to be an image of his own eternity.
text.NABRE: For God formed us to be imperishable; the image of his own nature he made us.
text.NRSV: for God created us for incorruption, and made us in the image of his own eternity,
text.DRB: For God created man incorruptible, and to the image of his own likeness he made him.

text.KJV: Nevertheless through envy of the devil came death into the world: and they that do hold of his side do find it.
text.NABRE: But by the envy of the devil, death entered the world, and they who are allied with him experience it.
text.NRSV: but through the devil’s envy death entered the world, and those who belong to his company experience it.
text.DRB: But by the envy of the devil, death entered into the world:
and they follow him that are of his part.

text.KJV: But the souls of the righteous are in the hand of God, and there shall no torment touch them.
text.NABRE: Chapter 3 The Hidden Counsels of God A. On Suffering The souls of the righteous are in the hand of God, and no torment shall touch them.
text.NRSV: The Destiny of the Righteous 3 and no torment will ever touch them.
text.DRB: But the souls of the just are in the hand of God, and the torment of death shall not touch them.

text.KJV: In the sight of the unwise they seemed to die: and their departure is taken for misery,
text.NABRE: They seemed, in the view of the foolish, to be dead; and their passing away was thought an affliction
text.NRSV: In the eyes of the foolish they seemed to have died, and their departure was thought to be a disaster,
text.DRB: They seemed in the eyes of the unwise to die: and their decease was counted affliction:

text.KJV: And their going from us to be utter destruction: but they are in peace.
text.NABRE: and their going forth from us, utter destruction. But they are in peace.
text.NRSV: and their going from us to be their destruction; but they are at peace.
text.DRB: and that which with us is the way, is destruction: but they are in peace.

text.KJV: For though they be punished in the sight of men, yet is their hope full of immortality.
text.NABRE: For if to others, indeed, they seem punished, yet is their hope full of immortality;
text.NRSV: For though in the sight of others they were punished, their hope is full of immortality.
text.DRB: And though before men they suffered torments, their hope is full of immortality.

text.KJV: And having been a little chastised, they shall be greatly rewarded: for God proved them, and found them worthy for himself.
text.NABRE: Chastised a little, they shall be greatly blessed, because God tried them and found them worthy of himself.
text.NRSV: Having been disciplined a little, they will receive great good, because God tested them and found them worthy of himself;
text.DRB: Vexed in few things, in many they shall be well disposed of: because God hath tempted them, and hath found them worthy of himself.

text.KJV: As gold in the furnace hath he tried them, and received them as a burnt offering.
text.NABRE: As gold in the furnace, he proved them, and as sacrificial offerings he took them to himself.
text.NRSV: like gold in the furnace he tried them, and like a sacrificial burnt-offering he accepted them.
text.DRB: As gold in the furnace he hath proved them, and as an host of holocaust he hath received them, & in time there shall be respect of them.

text.KJV: And in the time of their visitation they shall shine, and run to and fro like sparks among the stubble.
text.NABRE: In the time of their judgment they shall shine and dart about as sparks through stubble;
text.NRSV: In the time of their visitation they will shine forth, and will run like sparks through the stubble.
text.DRB: The just shall shine, and as sparks in a place of reeds they shall run abroad.

text.KJV: They shall judge the nations, and have dominion over the people, and their Lord shall reign for ever.
text.NABRE: They shall judge nations and rule over peoples, and the Lord shall be their King forever.
text.NRSV: They will govern nations and rule over peoples, and the Lord will reign over them for ever.
text.DRB: They shall judge nations, & have dominion over peoples, and their Lord shall reign for ever.

text.KJV: They that put their trust in him shall understand the truth: and such as be faithful in love shall abide with him: for grace and mercy is to his saints, and he hath care for his elect.
text.NABRE: Those who trust in him shall understand truth, and the faithful shall abide with him in love: Because grace and mercy are with his holy ones, and his care is with the elect.
text.NRSV: Those who trust in him will understand truth, and the faithful will abide with him in love, because grace and mercy are upon his holy ones, and he watches over his elect.
text.DRB: They that trust in him, shall understand truth: & the faithful in love shall rest in him: because rest and peace is to his elect.
But the impious according to the things which they have thought, shall have correption: which have neglected the just, & have revolted from our Lord.
text.KJV: But the ungodly shall be punished according to their own imaginations, which have neglected the righteous, and forsaken the Lord.
text.NABRE: But the wicked shall receive a punishment to match their thoughts, since they neglected righteousness and forsook the Lord.
text.NRSV: The Destiny of the Ungodly But the ungodly will be punished as their reasoning deserves, those who disregarded the righteous and rebelled against the Lord;

text.DRB: For he that rejecteth wisdom and discipline, is unhappy: and their hope is vain, and labours without fruit, & their works unprofitable.
text.KJV: For whoso despiseth wisdom and nurture, he is miserable, and their hope is vain, their labours unfruitful, and their works unprofitable:
text.NABRE: For those who despise wisdom and instruction are doomed. Vain is their hope, fruitless their labors, and worthless their works.
text.NRSV: for those who despise wisdom and instruction are miserable. Their hope is vain, their labours are unprofitable, and their works are useless.

text.DRB: Their wives are senseless, and their children most wicked.
text.KJV: Their wives are foolish, and their children wicked:
text.NABRE: Their wives are foolish and their children wicked, accursed their brood.
text.NRSV: Their wives are foolish, and their children evil;

text.DRB: Cursed is their creature: because happy is the barren woman: and the undefiled, which hath not known bed in sin, she shall have fruit in visitation of holy souls:
text.KJV: Their offspring is cursed. Wherefore blessed is the barren that is undefiled, which hath not known the sinful bed: she shall have fruit in the visitation of souls.
text.NABRE: B. On Childlessness Yes, blessed is she who, childless and undefiled, never knew transgression of the marriage bed; for she shall bear fruit at the judgment of souls.
text.NRSV: their offspring are accursed. On Childlessness For blessed is the barren woman who is undefiled, who has not entered into a sinful union; she will have fruit when God examines souls.

text.DRB: and the eunuch that hath not wrought iniquity with his hands, nor thought most wicked things against God: for the chosen gift of faith shall be given to him, and a most acceptable lot in the temple of God.
text.KJV: And blessed is the eunuch, which with his hands hath wrought no iniquity, nor imagined wicked things against God: for unto him shall be given the special gift of faith, and an inheritance in the temple of the Lord more acceptable to his mind.
text.NABRE: So also the eunuch whose hand wrought no misdeed, who held no wicked thoughts against the Lord — For he shall be given fidelity’s choice reward and a more gratifying heritage in the Lord ’s temple.
text.NRSV: Blessed also is the eunuch whose hands have done no lawless deed, and who has not devised wicked things against the Lord; for special favour will be shown him for his faithfulness, and a place of great delight in the temple of the Lord.

text.DRB: For of good labour there is glorious fruit, and the root of wisdom which falleth not.
text.KJV: For glorious is the fruit of good labours: and the root of wisdom shall never fall away.
text.NABRE: For the fruit of noble struggles is a glorious one; and unfailing is the root of understanding.
text.NRSV: For the fruit of good labours is renowned, and the root of understanding does not fail.

text.DRB: But the children of adulterers shall be in consummation, and the seed of the unlawful bed shall be destroyed.
text.KJV: As for the children of adulterers, they shall not come to their perfection, and the seed of an unrighteous bed shall be rooted out.
text.NABRE: But the children of adulterers will remain without issue, and the progeny of an unlawful bed will disappear.
text.NRSV: But children of adulterers will not come to maturity, and the offspring of an unlawful union will perish.

text.DRB: And if certes they be of long life, they shall be reputed for nothing, & their last old-age shall be without honour.
text.KJV: For though they live long, yet shall they be nothing regarded: and their last age shall be without honour.
text.NABRE: For should they attain long life, they will be held in no esteem, and dishonored will their old age be in the end;
text.NRSV: Even if they live long they will be held of no account, and finally their old age will be without honour.

text.DRB: And if they die quickly they shall have no hope, nor speech of comfort in the day of acknowledging.
text.KJV: Or, if they die quickly, they have no hope, neither comfort in the day of trial.
text.NABRE: Should they die abruptly, they will have no hope nor comfort in the day of scrutiny;
text.NRSV: If they die young, they will have no hope and no consolation on the day of judgement.

text.DRB: For of a wicked nation the ends are cruel.
text.KJV: For horrible is the end of the unrighteous generation.
text.NABRE: for dire is the end of the wicked generation.
text.NRSV: For the end of an unrighteous generation is grievous.

text.KJV: Better it is to have no children, and to have virtue: for the memorial thereof is immortal: because it is known with God, and with men.
text.NABRE: Chapter 4 Better is childlessness with virtue; for immortal is the memory of virtue, acknowledged both by God and human beings.
text.NRSV: 4 for in the memory of virtue is immortality, because it is known both by God and by mortals.
text.DRB: Ohow beautiful is the chaste generation with glory! For the memory thereof is immortal: because it is known both with God and with men.
When it is present, they imitate it, and they desire it when it hath withdrawn it-self, and it triumpheth crowned for ever, winning the reward of undefiled conflicts.
text.KJV: When it is present, men take example at it; and when it is gone, they desire it: it weareth a crown, and triumpheth for ever, having gotten the victory, striving for undefiled rewards.
text.NABRE: When it is present people imitate it, and they long for it when it is gone; Forever it marches crowned in triumph, victorious in unsullied deeds of valor.
text.NRSV: When it is present, people imitate it, and they long for it when it has gone; throughout all time it marches, crowned in triumph, victor in the contest for prizes that are undefiled.

text.DRB: But the multitude of the impious, that hath many children, shall not be profitable, and bastard plants shall not take deep root, nor lay sure foundation.
text.KJV: But the multiplying brood of the ungodly shall not thrive, nor take deep rooting from bastard slips, nor lay any fast foundation.
text.NABRE: But the numerous progeny of the wicked shall be of no avail; their spurious offshoots shall not strike deep root nor take firm hold.
text.NRSV: But the prolific brood of the ungodly will be of no use, and none of their illegitimate seedlings will strike a deep root or take a firm hold.

text.DRB: And if in the boughs for a time they shall spring being weakly set, they shall be moved of the wind, and by the vehemency of the winds they shall be rooted out.
text.KJV: For though they flourish in branches for a time; yet standing not last, they shall be shaken with the wind, and through the force of winds they shall be rooted out.
text.NABRE: For even though their branches flourish for a time, they are unsteady and shall be rocked by the wind and, by the violence of the winds, uprooted;
text.NRSV: For even if they put forth boughs for a while, standing insecurely they will be shaken by the wind, and by the violence of the winds they will be uprooted.

text.DRB: For the unperfect boughs shall be broken, and their fruits shall be unprofitable, and sour to eat, and meet for nothing.
text.KJV: The imperfect branches shall be broken off, their fruit unprofitable, not ripe to eat, yea, meet for nothing.
text.NABRE: Their twigs shall be broken off untimely, their fruit useless, unripe for eating, fit for nothing.
text.NRSV: The branches will be broken off before they come to maturity, and their fruit will be useless, not ripe enough to eat, and good for nothing.

text.DRB: For the children that be born of wicked sleeps, are witnesses of wickedness against the parents in their examination.
text.KJV: For children begotten of unlawful beds are witnesses of wickedness against their parents in their trial.
text.NABRE: For children born of lawless unions give evidence of the wickedness of their parents, when they are examined.
text.NRSV: For children born of unlawful unions are witnesses of evil against their parents when God examines them.

text.KJV: But though the righteous be prevented with death, yet shall he be in rest.
text.NABRE: C. On Early Death But the righteous one, though he die early, shall be at rest.
text.NRSV: But the righteous, though they die early, will be at rest.
text.DRB: But the just if he be prevented with death, shall be in a place of refreshing.

text.KJV: For honourable age is not that which standeth in length of time, nor that is measured by number of years.
text.NABRE: For the age that is honorable comes not with the passing of time, nor can it be measured in terms of years.
text.NRSV: For old age is not honoured for length of time, or measured by number of years;
text.DRB: For venerable old age is not that of long time, nor accounted by the number of years: but the understanding of a man are grey hairs:

text.KJV: But wisdom is the gray hair unto men, and an unspotted life is old age.
text.NABRE: Rather, understanding passes for gray hair, and an unsullied life is the attainment of old age.
text.NRSV: but understanding is grey hair for anyone, and a blameless life is ripe old age.
text.DRB: and an immaculate life is old age.

text.KJV: He pleased God, and was beloved of him: so that living among sinners he was translated.
text.NABRE: The one who pleased God was loved, living among sinners, was transported—
text.NRSV: There were some who pleased God and were loved by him, and while living among sinners were taken up.
text.DRB: Pleasing God he is made beloved, and living among sinners he was translated.

text.KJV: Yea speedily was he taken away, lest that wickedness should alter his understanding, or deceit beguile his soul.
text.NABRE: Snatched away, lest wickedness pervert his mind or deceit beguile his soul;
text.NRSV: They were caught up so that evil might not change their understanding or guile deceive their souls.
text.DRB: He was taken away lest malice should change his understanding, or lest any guile might deceive his soul.
For the bewitching of vanity obscureth good things, and the inconstancy of concupiscence perverteth the understanding that is without malice.
text.KJV: For the bewitching of naughtiness doth obscure things that are honest; and the wandering of concupiscence doth undermine the simple mind.
text.NABRE: For the witchery of paltry things obscures what is right and the whirl of desire transforms the innocent mind.
text.NRSV: For the fascination of wickedness obscures what is good, and roving desire perverts the innocent mind.

text.DRB: Being consummate in short space he fulfilled much time:
text.KJV: He, being made perfect in a short time, fulfilled a long time:
text.NABRE: Having become perfect in a short while, he reached the fullness of a long career;
text.NRSV: Being perfected in a short time, they fulfilled long years;

text.DRB: for his soul pleased God: for this cause he hastened to bring him out of the midst of iniquities: but the peoples that are seeing, and not understanding, nor putting such things in their hearts:
text.KJV: For his soul pleased the Lord: therefore hasted he to take him away from among the wicked.
text.NABRE: for his soul was pleasing to the Lord, therefore he sped him out of the midst of wickedness. But the people saw and did not understand, nor did they take that consideration into account.
text.NRSV: for their souls were pleasing to the Lord, therefore he took them quickly from the midst of wickedness.

text.DRB: that the grace of God, and mercy is toward his saints, and respect toward his elect.
text.KJV: This the people saw, and understood it not, neither laid they up this in their minds, That his grace and mercy is with his saints, and that he hath respect unto his chosen.
text.NRSV: Yet the peoples saw and did not understand, or take such a thing to heart, that God’s grace and mercy are with his elect, and that he watches over his holy ones.

text.DRB: But the just dead condemneth the impious alive, and youth soon ended, the long life of the unjust.
text.KJV: Thus the righteous that is dead shall condemn the ungodly which are living; and youth that is soon perfected the many years and old age of the unrighteous.
text.NRSV: The Triumph of the Righteous The righteous who have died will condemn the ungodly who are living, and youth that is quickly perfected will condemn the prolonged old age of the unrighteous.

text.DRB: For they shall see the end of the wise, and shall not understand what God hath thought of him, and why our Lord hath fenced him.
text.KJV: For they shall see the end of the wise, and shall not understand what God in his counsel hath decreed of him, and to what end the Lord hath set him in safety.
text.NRSV: For they will see the end of the wise, and will not understand what the Lord purposed for them, and for what he kept them safe.

text.DRB: For they shall see and shall contemn him: but our Lord shall laugh them to scorn,
text.KJV: They shall see him, and despise him; but God shall laugh them to scorn: and they shall hereafter be a vile carcase, and a reproach among the dead for evermore.
text.NRSV: The unrighteous will see, and will have contempt for them, but the Lord will laugh them to scorn. After this they will become dishonoured corpses, and an outrage among the dead for ever;

text.DRB: and they shall fall after this without honour, and in contumely among the dead for ever: because he shall break them puffed up without voice, and shall remove them from the foundations, and they shall be made desolate unto the highest degree: and shall be mourning, and the memory of them shall perish.
text.KJV: For he shall rend them, and cast them down headlong, that they shall be speechless; and he shall shake them from the foundation; and they shall be utterly laid waste, and be in sorrow; and their memorial shall perish.
text.NRSV: because he will dash them speechless to the ground, and shake them from the foundations; they will be left utterly dry and barren, and they will suffer anguish, and the memory of them will perish.

text.DRB: They shall come fearful in cogitation of their sins, and their iniquities on the contrary shall convince them.
text.KJV: And when they cast up the accounts of their sins, they shall come with fear: and their own iniquities shall convince them to their face.
text.NRSV: The Final Judgement They will come with dread when their sins are reckoned up, and their lawless deeds will convict them to their face.

text.KJV: Then shall the righteous man stand in great boldness before the face of such as have afflicted him, and made no account of his labours.
text.NABRE: Chapter 5 Then shall the righteous one with great assurance confront his oppressors who set at nought his labors.
text.NRSV: 5 in the presence of those who have oppressed them and those who make light of their labours.
text.DRB: Then shall the just stand in great constancy against those that have afflicted them, and taken away their labours.

text.KJV: When they see it, they shall be troubled with terrible fear, and shall be amazed at the strangeness of his salvation, so far beyond all that they looked for.
text.NABRE: Seeing this, the wicked shall be shaken with dreadful fear, and be amazed at the unexpected salvation.
text.NRSV: When the unrighteous see them, they will be shaken with dreadful fear, and they will be amazed at the unexpected salvation of the righteous.
text.DRB: They seeing shall be troubled with horrible fear, and shall marvel at the suddenness of unexpected salvation,
saying within themselves, repenting, and sighing for anguish of spirit: These are they, whom we had sometime in derision, and in a parable of reproach.
text.KJV: And they repenting and groaning for anguish of spirit shall say within themselves, This was he, whom we had sometimes in derision, and a proverb of reproach:
text.NABRE: They shall say among themselves, rueful and groaning through anguish of spirit: “This is the one whom once we held as a laughingstock and as a type for mockery,
text.NRSV: They will speak to one another in repentance, and in anguish of spirit they will groan, and say,

text.KJV: We fools accounted his life madness, and his end to be without honour:
text.NABRE: fools that we were! His life we accounted madness, and death dishonored.
text.NRSV: ‘These are persons whom we once held in derision and made a byword of reproach—fools that we were! We thought that their lives were madness and that their end was without honour.
text.DRB: We senseless esteemed their life madness, and their end without honour.

text.KJV: How is he numbered among the children of God, and his lot is among the saints!
text.NABRE: See how he is accounted among the heavenly beings; how his lot is with the holy ones!
text.NRSV: Why have they been numbered among the children of God? And why is their lot among the saints?
text.DRB: Behold how they are counted among the children of God, and their lot is among the saints.
We therefore have erred from the way of truth, and the light of justice hath not shined to us, and the sun of understanding rose not to us.
text.KJV: Therefore have we erred from the way of truth, and the light of righteousness hath not shined unto us, and the sun of righteousness rose not upon us.
text.NABRE: We, then, have strayed from the way of truth, and the light of righteousness did not shine for us, and the sun did not rise for us.
text.NRSV: So it was we who strayed from the way of truth, and the light of righteousness did not shine on us, and the sun did not rise upon us.

text.DRB: We are wearied in the way of iniquity and perdition, and have walked hard ways, but the way of our Lord we have not known.
text.KJV: We wearied ourselves in the way of wickedness and destruction: yea, we have gone through deserts, where there lay no way: but as for the way of the Lord, we have not known it.
text.NABRE: We were entangled in the thorns of mischief and of ruin; we journeyed through trackless deserts, but the way of the Lord we never knew.
text.NRSV: We took our fill of the paths of lawlessness and destruction, and we journeyed through trackless deserts, but the way of the Lord we have not known.

text.DRB: What hath pride profited us? Or what commodity hath the vaunting of riches brought to us?
text.KJV: What hath pride profited us? or what good hath riches with our vaunting brought us?
text.NABRE: What did our pride avail us? What have wealth and its boastfulness afforded us?
text.NRSV: What has our arrogance profited us? And what good has our boasted wealth brought us?

text.DRB: All those things are passed away as a shadow, and as a messenger running before,
text.KJV: All those things are passed away like a shadow, and as a post that hasted by;
text.NABRE: All of them passed like a shadow and like a fleeting rumor;
text.NRSV: ‘All those things have vanished like a shadow, and like a rumour that passes by;

text.DRB: and as a ship, that passeth through the surging waters: whereof, when it is past, the trace cannot be found nor the path of that ship's keel in the waves:
text.KJV: And as a ship that passeth over the waves of the water, which when it is gone by, the trace thereof cannot be found, neither the pathway of the keel in the waves;
text.NABRE: Like a ship traversing the heaving water: when it has passed, no trace can be found, no path of its keel in the waves.
text.NRSV: like a ship that sails through the billowy water, and when it has passed no trace can be found, no track of its keel in the waves;

text.DRB: or as a bird, that flyeth through in the air, of which there is no token can be found of her passage, but only a sound of the wings beating the light wind: and by vehemence of going cutting the air, moving the wings she is flown through, and afterward there is no sign found of her way:
text.KJV: Or as when a bird hath flown through the air, there is no token of her way to be found, but the light air being beaten with the stroke of her wings and parted with the violent noise and motion of them, is passed through, and therein afterwards no sign where she went is to be found;
text.NABRE: Or like a bird flying through the air; no evidence of its course is to be found— But the fluid air, lashed by the beating of pinions, and cleft by the rushing force Of speeding wings, is traversed; and afterward no mark of passage can be found in it.
text.NRSV: or as, when a bird flies through the air, no evidence of its passage is found; the light air, lashed by the beat of its pinions and pierced by the force of its rushing flight, is traversed by the movement of its wings, and afterwards no sign of its coming is found there;

text.DRB: or as when an arrow is shot forth to a set mark, the divided air is forthwith closed in it-self again, so that the passage thereof is not known:
text.KJV: Or like as when an arrow is shot at a mark, it parteth the air, which immediately cometh together again, so that a man cannot know where it went through:
text.NABRE: Or as, when an arrow has been shot at a mark, the parted air straightway flows together again so that none discerns the way it went—
text.NRSV: or as, when an arrow is shot at a target, the air, thus divided, comes together at once, so that no one knows its pathway.

text.DRB: so we also being born forthwith ceased to be: and of virtue certes have been able to shew no sign: but in our naughtiness we are consumed.
text.KJV: Even so we in like manner, as soon as we were born, began to draw to our end, and had no sign of virtue to shew; but were consumed in our own wickedness.
text.NABRE: Even so, once born, we abruptly came to nought and held no sign of virtue to display, but were consumed in our wickedness.”
text.NRSV: So we also, as soon as we were born, ceased to be, and we had no sign of virtue to show, but were consumed in our wickedness.’

text.DRB: Such things said they in hell, which sinned:
text.KJV: For the hope of the Godly is like dust that is blown away with the wind; like a thin froth that is driven away with the storm; like as the smoke which is dispersed here and there with a tempest, and passeth away as the remembrance of a guest that tarrieth but a day.
text.NABRE: Yes, the hope of the wicked is like chaff borne by the wind, and like fine, storm-driven snow; Like smoke scattered by the wind, and like the passing memory of the nomad camping for a single day.
text.NRSV: Because the hope of the ungodly is like thistledown carried by the wind, and like a light frost driven away by a storm; it is dispersed like smoke before the wind, and it passes like the remembrance of a guest who stays but a day.

text.KJV: But the righteous live for evermore; their reward also is with the Lord, and the care of them is with the most High.
text.NABRE: But the righteous live forever, and in the Lord is their recompense, and the thought of them is with the Most High.
text.NRSV: The Reward of the Righteous But the righteous live for ever, and their reward is with the Lord; the Most High takes care of them.
text.DRB: because the hope of the impious is as dust, which is taken away with the wind: and as a thin froth, which is dispersed by the storm: and as smoke that is scattered abroad by the wind: and as the memory of a guest of one day that passeth.

text.KJV: Therefore shall they receive a glorious kingdom, and a beautiful crown from the Lord’s hand: for with his right hand shall he cover them, and with his arm shall he protect them.
text.NABRE: Therefore shall they receive the splendid crown, the beautiful diadem, from the hand of the Lord, For he will shelter them with his right hand, and protect them with his arm.
text.NRSV: Therefore they will receive a glorious crown and a beautiful diadem from the hand of the Lord, because with his right hand he will cover them, and with his arm he will shield them.
text.DRB: But the just shall live for ever, and their reward is with our Lord, and cogitation of them with the Highest.
Therefore shall they receive a Kingdom of honour, & a crown of beauty at the hand of our Lord: because with his right hand he will cover them, and within his holy arm he will defend them.
text.KJV: He shall take to him his jealousy for complete armour, and make the creature his weapon for the revenge of his enemies.
text.NABRE: He shall take his zeal for armor and arm creation to requite the enemy,
text.NRSV: The Lord will take his zeal as his whole armour, and will arm all creation to repel his enemies;

text.DRB: And his zeal will take armour, and he will arm the creature to the revenge of the enemies.
text.KJV: He shall put on righteousness as a breastplate, and true judgment instead of an helmet.
text.NABRE: Shall put on righteousness for a breastplate, wear sure judgment for a helmet,
text.NRSV: he will put on righteousness as a breastplate, and wear impartial justice as a helmet;

text.DRB: He will put on justice for a breast-plate, & will take sincere judgement for an helmet:
text.KJV: He shall take holiness for an invincible shield.
text.NABRE: Shall take invincible holiness for a shield,
text.NRSV: he will take holiness as an invincible shield,

text.DRB: he will take equity for an invincible shield:
text.KJV: His severe wrath shall he sharpen for a sword, and the world shall fight with him against the unwise.
text.NABRE: and sharpen his sudden anger for a sword. The universe will war with him against the foolhardy;
text.NRSV: and sharpen stern wrath for a sword, and creation will join with him to fight against his frenzied foes.

text.DRB: and he will sharpen fierce wrath for a spear, & the round world shall fight with him against the senseless.
text.KJV: Then shall the right aiming thunderbolts go abroad; and from the clouds, as from a well drawn bow, shall they fly to the mark.
text.NABRE: Well-aimed bolts of lightning will go forth and from the clouds will leap to the mark as from a well-drawn bow;
text.NRSV: Shafts of lightning will fly with true aim, and will leap from the clouds to the target, as from a well-drawn bow,

text.DRB: The shots of lightnings shall go directly, & as it were from a bow of the clouds well bent they shall be cast forth, and shall light on a certain place.
text.KJV: And hailstones full of wrath shall be cast as out of a stone bow, and the water of the sea shall rage against them, and the floods shall cruelly drown them.
text.NABRE: and as from a sling, wrathful hailstones shall be hurled. The waters of the sea will be enraged and flooding rivers will overwhelm them;
text.NRSV: and hailstones full of wrath will be hurled as from a catapult; the water of the sea will rage against them, and rivers will relentlessly overwhelm them;

text.DRB: And from rocked wrath shall thick hail-stones be cast, the water of the sea shall rage against them, and the rivers shall run together roughly.
text.KJV: Yea, a mighty wind shall stand up against them, and like a storm shall blow them away: thus iniquity shall lay waste the whole earth, and ill dealing shall overthrow the thrones of the mighty.
text.NABRE: A mighty wind will confront them and winnow them like a tempest; Thus lawlessness will lay waste the whole earth and evildoing overturn the thrones of the mighty.
text.NRSV: a mighty wind will rise against them, and like a tempest it will winnow them away. Lawlessness will lay waste the whole earth, and evildoing will overturn the thrones of rulers.

text.DRB: Aspirit of power shall stand against them, and as a hurlwind shall divide them: and their iniquity shall bring all the land to a desert, and naughtiness shall overthrow the seats of the mighty.

text.KJV: Hear therefore, O ye kings, and understand; learn, ye that be judges of the ends of the earth.
text.NABRE: Chapter 6 Exhortation to Seek Wisdom Hear, therefore, kings, and understand; learn, you magistrates of the earth’s expanse!
text.NRSV: Kings Should Seek Wisdom 6 learn, O judges of the ends of the earth.
text.DRB: Wisdom is better than strength: and a wise man than a strong.
Hear therefore ye kings, & understand, learn ye judges of the ends of the earth.
text.KJV: Give ear, ye that rule the people, and glory in the multitude of nations.
text.NABRE: Give ear, you who have power over multitudes and lord it over throngs of peoples!
text.NRSV: Give ear, you that rule over multitudes, and boast of many nations.

text.KJV: For power is given you of the Lord, and sovereignty from the Highest, who shall try your works, and search out your counsels.
text.NABRE: Because authority was given you by the Lord and sovereignty by the Most High, who shall probe your works and scrutinize your counsels!
text.NRSV: For your dominion was given you from the Lord, and your sovereignty from the Most High; he will search out your works and inquire into your plans.
text.DRB: Give ear ye that rule multitudes, and that please yourselves in multitudes of nations:
because the power is given you of our Lord, and strength by the Highest, who will examine your works, and search your cogitations:
text.KJV: Because, being ministers of his kingdom, ye have not judged aright, nor kept the law, nor walked after the counsel of God;
text.NABRE: Because, though you were ministers of his kingdom, you did not judge rightly, and did not keep the law, nor walk according to the will of God,
text.NRSV: Because as servants of his kingdom you did not rule rightly, or keep the law, or walk according to the purpose of God,

text.DRB: because when you were the ministers of his Kingdom, you judged not rightly, nor kept the law of justice, nor have walked according to the will of God.
text.KJV: Horribly and speedily shall he come upon you: for a sharp judgment shall be to them that be in high places.
text.NABRE: Terribly and swiftly he shall come against you, because severe judgment awaits the exalted—
text.NRSV: he will come upon you terribly and swiftly, because severe judgement falls on those in high places.

text.DRB: Horribly and quickly will he appear to you: because most severe judgement shall be done on them, that bear rule.
text.KJV: For mercy will soon pardon the meanest: but mighty men shall be mightily tormented.
text.NABRE: For the lowly may be pardoned out of mercy but the mighty shall be mightily put to the test.
text.NRSV: For the lowliest may be pardoned in mercy, but the mighty will be mightily tested.

text.DRB: For to the little one mercy is granted: but the mighty shall mightily suffer torments.
text.KJV: For he which is Lord over all shall fear no man’s person, neither shall he stand in awe of any man’s greatness: for he hath made the small and great, and careth for all alike.
text.NABRE: For the Ruler of all shows no partiality, nor does he fear greatness, Because he himself made the great as well as the small, and provides for all alike;
text.NRSV: For the Lord of all will not stand in awe of anyone, or show deference to greatness; because he himself made both small and great, and he takes thought for all alike.

text.DRB: For God will not except any man's person, neither will fear the greatness of any man: because he made the little and the great, & he hath equally care of all.
text.KJV: But a sore trial shall come upon the mighty.
text.NABRE: but for those in power a rigorous scrutiny impends.
text.NRSV: But a strict inquiry is in store for the mighty.

text.DRB: But to the stronger more strong torment is imminent.
text.KJV: Unto you therefore, O kings, do I speak, that ye may learn wisdom, and not fall away.
text.NABRE: To you, therefore, O princes, are my words addressed that you may learn wisdom and that you may not fall away.
text.NRSV: To you then, O monarchs, my words are directed, so that you may learn wisdom and not transgress.

text.DRB: To you therefore o kings are these my words, that you may learn wisdom, and not fall.
text.KJV: For they that keep holiness holily shall be judged holy: and they that have learned such things shall find what to answer.
text.NABRE: For those who keep the holy precepts hallowed will be found holy, and those learned in them will have ready a response.
text.NRSV: For they will be made holy who observe holy things in holiness, and those who have been taught them will find a defence.

text.DRB: For they that have kept just things justly, shall be justified: and they that have learned these things, shall find what they may answer.
text.KJV: Wherefore set your affection upon my words; desire them, and ye shall be instructed.
text.NABRE: Desire therefore my words; long for them and you will be instructed.
text.NRSV: Therefore set your desire on my words; long for them, and you will be instructed.

text.KJV: Wisdom is glorious, and never fadeth away: yea, she is easily seen of them that love her, and found of such as seek her.
text.NABRE: Resplendent and unfading is Wisdom, and she is readily perceived by those who love her, and found by those who seek her.
text.NRSV: Description of Wisdom Wisdom is radiant and unfading, and she is easily discerned by those who love her, and is found by those who seek her.
text.DRB: Covet ye therefore my words, and love them, and you shall have discipline.
Wisdom is clear, and such as never fadeth, and is easily seen of them that love her, and is found of them that seek her.
text.KJV: She preventeth them that desire her, in making herself first known unto them.
text.NABRE: She hastens to make herself known to those who desire her;
text.NRSV: She hastens to make herself known to those who desire her.

text.DRB: She preventeth them that covet her, that she first may shew herself unto them.
text.KJV: Whoso seeketh her early shall have no great travail: for he shall find her sitting at his doors.
text.NABRE: one who watches for her at dawn will not be disappointed, for she will be found sitting at the gate.
text.NRSV: One who rises early to seek her will have no difficulty, for she will be found sitting at the gate.

text.DRB: He that awaketh early to her, shall not labour: for he shall find her sitting at her doors.
text.KJV: To think therefore upon her is perfection of wisdom: and whoso watcheth for her shall quickly be without care.
text.NABRE: For setting your heart on her is the perfection of prudence, and whoever keeps vigil for her is quickly free from care;
text.NRSV: To fix one’s thought on her is perfect understanding, and one who is vigilant on her account will soon be free from care,

text.DRB: To think therefore of her, is perfect understanding: and he that watcheth for her, shall quickly be secure.
text.KJV: For she goeth about seeking such as are worthy of her, sheweth herself favourably unto them in the ways, and meeteth them in every thought.
text.NABRE: Because she makes her rounds, seeking those worthy of her, and graciously appears to them on the way, and goes to meet them with full attention.
text.NRSV: because she goes about seeking those worthy of her, and she graciously appears to them in their paths, and meets them in every thought.

text.KJV: For the very true beginning of her is the desire of discipline; and the care of discipline is love;
text.NABRE: For the first step toward Wisdom is an earnest desire for discipline;
text.NRSV: The beginning of wisdom is the most sincere desire for instruction, and concern for instruction is love of her,
text.DRB: Because she goeth about seeking them that be worthy of her, and in the ways she will shew herself to them cheerfully, and in all providence she will meet them.

text.KJV: And love is the keeping of her laws; and the giving heed unto her laws is the assurance of incorruption;
text.NABRE: then, care for discipline is love of her; love means the keeping of her laws; To observe her laws is the basis for incorruptibility;
text.NRSV: and love of her is the keeping of her laws, and giving heed to her laws is assurance of immortality,
text.DRB: For the beginning of her is the most true desire of discipline.

text.KJV: And incorruption maketh us near unto God:
text.NABRE: and incorruptibility makes one close to God;
text.NRSV: and immortality brings one near to God;
text.DRB: The care therefore of discipline, is love: and love is keeping of her laws: and the keeping of the laws, is the consummation of incorruption:

text.KJV: Therefore the desire of wisdom bringeth to a kingdom.
text.NABRE: thus the desire for Wisdom leads to a kingdom.
text.NRSV: so the desire for wisdom leads to a kingdom.
text.DRB: and incorruption maketh to be next to God.
Therefore the desire of wisdom leadeth to the everlasting Kingdom.
text.KJV: If your delight be then in thrones and sceptres, O ye kings of the people, honour wisdom, that ye may reign for evermore.
text.NABRE: If, then, you find pleasure in throne and scepter, you princes of peoples, honor Wisdom, that you may reign as kings forever.
text.NRSV: Therefore if you delight in thrones and sceptres, O monarchs over the peoples, honour wisdom, so that you may reign for ever.

text.DRB: If therefore you be delighted with thrones, and with sceptres, o ye kings of the people, love wisdom, that you may reign for ever.
text.KJV: As for wisdom, what she is, and how she came up, I will tell you, and will not hide mysteries from you: but will seek her out from the beginning of her nativity, and bring the knowledge of her into light, and will not pass over the truth.
text.NABRE: II. Praise of Wisdom by Solomon Introduction Now what wisdom is, and how she came to be I shall proclaim; and I shall conceal no secrets from you, But from the very beginning I shall search out and bring to light knowledge of her; I shall not diverge from the truth.
text.NRSV: I will tell you what wisdom is and how she came to be, and I will hide no secrets from you, but I will trace her course from the beginning of creation, and make knowledge of her clear, and I will not pass by the truth;

text.DRB: Love the light of wisdom all ye that bear rule over peoples.
text.KJV: Neither will I go with consuming envy; for such a man shall have no fellowship with wisdom.
text.NABRE: Neither shall I admit consuming jealousy to my company, because that can have no fellowship with Wisdom.
text.NRSV: nor will I travel in the company of sickly envy, for envy does not associate with wisdom.

text.KJV: But the multitude of the wise is the welfare of the world: and a wise king is the upholding of the people.
text.NABRE: A multitude of the wise is the safety of the world, and a prudent king, the stability of the people;
text.NRSV: The multitude of the wise is the salvation of the world, and a sensible king is the stability of any people.
text.DRB: But what wisdom is, and how she was made, I will declare: and I will not hide from you the mysteries of God, but from the beginning of her nativity I will search out, and set the knowledge of her into light, and will not let pass the truth:
neither will I go with pining envy: because such a man shall not be partaker of wisdom.
text.KJV: Receive therefore instruction through my words, and it shall do you good.
text.NABRE: so take instruction from my words, to your profit.
text.NRSV: Therefore be instructed by my words, and you will profit.

text.DRB: But the multitude of the wise is the health of the round world: and a wise king is the stability of the people.
Therefore take ye discipline by my words, and it shall profit you.

text.KJV: I myself also am a mortal man, like to all, and the offspring of him that was first made of the earth,
text.NABRE: Chapter 7 Solomon Is Like All Others I too am a mortal, the same as all the rest, and a descendant of the first one formed of earth. And in my mother’s womb I was molded into flesh
text.NRSV: Solomon Like Other Mortals 7 a descendant of the first-formed child of earth; and in the womb of a mother I was moulded into flesh,
text.DRB: I also certes am a mortal man, like to all, and of the earthly kindred of him that was made first, and in the womb of my mother was I fashioned flesh,
the time of ten months was I brought together in blood, of the seed of man, and the delectation of sleep concurring.
text.KJV: And in my mother’s womb was fashioned to be flesh in the time of ten months, being compacted in blood, of the seed of man, and the pleasure that came with sleep.
text.NABRE: in a ten-month period —body and blood, from the seed of a man, and the pleasure that accompanies marriage.
text.NRSV: within the period of ten months, compacted with blood, from the seed of a man and the pleasure of marriage.

text.DRB: And I being born received the common air, and fell upon the earth that is made alike, and the first voice like to all men did I put forth weeping.
text.KJV: And when I was born, I drew in the common air, and fell upon the earth, which is of like nature, and the first voice which I uttered was crying, as all others do.
text.NABRE: And I too, when born, inhaled the common air, and fell upon the kindred earth; wailing, I uttered that first sound common to all.
text.NRSV: And when I was born, I began to breathe the common air, and fell upon the kindred earth; my first sound was a cry, as is true of all.

text.DRB: I was nourished in swaddling cloths, and great cares.
text.KJV: I was nursed in swaddling clothes, and that with cares.
text.NABRE: In swaddling clothes and with constant care I was nurtured.
text.NRSV: I was nursed with care in swaddling cloths.

text.DRB: For none of the kings had other beginning of nativity.
text.KJV: For there is no king that had any other beginning of birth.
text.NABRE: For no king has any different origin or birth;
text.NRSV: For no king has had a different beginning of existence;

text.DRB: There is one entrance therefore into life to all men, and like departure.
text.KJV: For all men have one entrance into life, and the like going out.
text.NABRE: one is the entry into life for all, and in one same way they leave it.
text.NRSV: there is for all one entrance into life, and one way out.

text.KJV: Wherefore I prayed, and understanding was given me: I called upon God, and the spirit of wisdom came to me.
text.NABRE: Solomon Prayed and Wisdom and Riches Came to Him Therefore I prayed, and prudence was given me; I pleaded and the spirit of Wisdom came to me.
text.NRSV: Solomon’s Respect for Wisdom Therefore I prayed, and understanding was given me; I called on God, and the spirit of wisdom came to me.
text.DRB: For this cause I wished, and understanding was given me: and I invocated, and the spirit of wisdom came upon me:

text.KJV: I preferred her before sceptres and thrones, and esteemed riches nothing in comparison of her.
text.NABRE: I preferred her to scepter and throne, And deemed riches nothing in comparison with her,
text.NRSV: I preferred her to sceptres and thrones, and I accounted wealth as nothing in comparison with her.
text.DRB: and I preferred her before Kingdoms and thrones, and riches I counted to be nothing in comparison of her.
Neither did I compare the precious stone to her: because all gold in comparison of her, is a little sand, and silver in the sight of her shall be esteemed as clay.
text.KJV: Neither compared I unto her any precious stone, because all gold in respect of her is as a little sand, and silver shall be counted as clay before her.
text.NABRE: nor did I liken any priceless gem to her; Because all gold, in view of her, is a bit of sand, and before her, silver is to be accounted mire.
text.NRSV: Neither did I liken to her any priceless gem, because all gold is but a little sand in her sight, and silver will be accounted as clay before her.

text.DRB: Above health and beauty did I love her, and purposed to have her for light: because her light cannot be extinguished.
text.KJV: I loved her above health and beauty, and chose to have her instead of light: for the light that cometh from her never goeth out.
text.NABRE: Beyond health and beauty I loved her, And I chose to have her rather than the light, because her radiance never ceases.
text.NRSV: I loved her more than health and beauty, and I chose to have her rather than light, because her radiance never ceases.

text.DRB: And all good things came to me together with her, and very much honesty by her hands,
text.KJV: All good things together came to me with her, and innumerable riches in her hands.
text.NABRE: Yet all good things together came to me with her, and countless riches at her hands;
text.NRSV: All good things came to me along with her, and in her hands uncounted wealth.

text.DRB: and I rejoiced in all: because this wisdom went before me, and I was ignorant that she is the mother of all these.
text.KJV: And I rejoiced in them all, because wisdom goeth before them: and I knew not that she was the mother of them.
text.NABRE: I rejoiced in them all, because Wisdom is their leader, though I had not known that she is their mother.
text.NRSV: I rejoiced in them all, because wisdom leads them; but I did not know that she was their mother.

text.DRB: Which I learned without fiction, and do communicate without envy, and her honesty I hid not.
text.KJV: I learned diligently, and do communicate her liberally: I do not hide her riches.
text.NABRE: Solomon Prays for Help to Speak Worthily of Wisdom Sincerely I learned about her, and ungrudgingly do I share— her riches I do not hide away;
text.NRSV: I learned without guile and I impart without grudging; I do not hide her wealth,

text.DRB: For she is an infinite treasure to men: which who so have, are made partakers of the friendship of God commended for the gifts of discipline.
text.KJV: For she is a treasure unto men that never faileth: which they that use become the friends of God, being commended for the gifts that come from learning.
text.NABRE: For she is an unfailing treasure; those who gain this treasure win the friendship of God, being commended by the gifts that come from her discipline.
text.NRSV: for it is an unfailing treasure for mortals; those who get it obtain friendship with God, commended for the gifts that come from instruction.

text.DRB: And to me God hath given to speak according to my mind, and to presume things worthy of those, that are given me: because he is the guide of wisdom, and the Creator of the wise:
text.KJV: God hath granted me to speak as I would, and to conceive as is meet for the things that are given me: because it is he that leadeth unto wisdom, and directeth the wise.
text.NABRE: Now God grant I speak suitably and value these endowments at their worth: For he is the guide of Wisdom and the director of the wise.
text.NRSV: Solomon Prays for Wisdom May God grant me to speak with judgement, and to have thoughts worthy of what I have received; for he is the guide even of wisdom and the corrector of the wise.

text.DRB: For in his hand are both we, & our words, and wisdom, and the knowledge & discipline of works.
text.KJV: For in his hand are both we and our words; all wisdom also, and knowledge of workmanship.
text.NABRE: For both we and our words are in his hand, as well as all prudence and knowledge of crafts.
text.NRSV: For both we and our words are in his hand, as are all understanding and skill in crafts.

text.DRB: For he gave me the true knowledge of those things which are: that I may know the disposition of the round world, and the virtues of the elements,
text.KJV: For he hath given me certain knowledge of the things that are, namely, to know how the world was made, and the operation of the elements:
text.NABRE: For he gave me sound knowledge of what exists, that I might know the structure of the universe and the force of its elements,
text.NRSV: For it is he who gave me unerring knowledge of what exists, to know the structure of the world and the activity of the elements;

text.DRB: the beginning, & end, & midst of times, the permutations of changeable seasons, and consummations of times,
text.KJV: The beginning, ending, and midst of the times: the alterations of the turning of the sun, and the change of seasons:
text.NABRE: The beginning and the end and the midpoint of times, the changes in the sun’s course and the variations of the seasons,
text.NRSV: the beginning and end and middle of times, the alternations of the solstices and the changes of the seasons,

text.DRB: the courses of the year, and dispositions of the stars,
text.KJV: The circuits of years, and the positions of stars:
text.NABRE: Cycles of years, positions of stars,
text.NRSV: the cycles of the year and the constellations of the stars,

text.DRB: the natures of beasts, and furies of wild beasts, the force of winds, and the cogitations of men, the differences of plants, and virtues of roots,
text.KJV: The natures of living creatures, and the furies of wild beasts: the violence of winds, and the reasonings of men: the diversities of plants and the virtues of roots:
text.NABRE: natures of living things, tempers of beasts, Powers of the winds and thoughts of human beings, uses of plants and virtues of roots—
text.NRSV: the natures of animals and the tempers of wild animals, the powers of spirits and the thoughts of human beings, the varieties of plants and the virtues of roots;

text.DRB: & whatsoever are hid things and not foreseen, I have learned: for wisdom the worker of all taught me.
text.KJV: And all such things as are either secret or manifest, them I know.
text.NABRE: Whatever is hidden or plain I learned,
text.NRSV: I learned both what is secret and what is manifest,

text.KJV: For wisdom, which is the worker of all things, taught me: for in her is an understanding spirit holy, one only, manifold, subtil, lively, clear, undefiled, plain, not subject to hurt, loving the thing that is good quick, which cannot be letted, ready to do good,
text.NABRE: for Wisdom, the artisan of all, taught me. Nature and Incomparable Dignity of Wisdom For in her is a spirit intelligent, holy, unique, Manifold, subtle, agile, clear, unstained, certain, Never harmful, loving the good, keen,
text.NRSV: for wisdom, the fashioner of all things, taught me. The Nature of Wisdom There is in her a spirit that is intelligent, holy, unique, manifold, subtle, mobile, clear, unpolluted, distinct, invulnerable, loving the good, keen, irresistible,
text.DRB: For in her is the spirit of understanding, holy, only, manifold, subtile, eloquent, moveable, undefiled, sure, sweet, loving, good, sharp, who nothing hindereth well-doing,
gentle, benign, stable, certain, secure, having all power, foreseeing all things, and that containeth all spirits: intelligible, clean, subtile.
text.KJV: Kind to man, steadfast, sure, free from care, having all power, overseeing all things, and going through all understanding, pure, and most subtil, spirits.
text.NABRE: unhampered, beneficent, kindly, Firm, secure, tranquil, all-powerful, all-seeing, And pervading all spirits, though they be intelligent, pure and very subtle.
text.NRSV: beneficent, humane, steadfast, sure, free from anxiety, all-powerful, overseeing all, and penetrating through all spirits that are intelligent, pure, and altogether subtle.

text.KJV: For wisdom is more moving than any motion: she passeth and goeth through all things by reason of her pureness.
text.NABRE: For Wisdom is mobile beyond all motion, and she penetrates and pervades all things by reason of her purity.
text.NRSV: For wisdom is more mobile than any motion; because of her pureness she pervades and penetrates all things.
text.DRB: For wisdom is more moveable than all moveable things: and reacheth every-where because of her cleanness.

text.KJV: For she is the breath of the power of God, and a pure influence flowing from the glory of the Almighty: therefore can no defiled thing fall into her.
text.NABRE: For she is a breath of the might of God and a pure emanation of the glory of the Almighty; therefore nothing defiled can enter into her.
text.NRSV: For she is a breath of the power of God, and a pure emanation of the glory of the Almighty; therefore nothing defiled gains entrance into her.
text.DRB: For she is a vapour of the power of God, & a certain sincere emanation of the glory of God omnipotent: and therefore no defiled thing cometh unto her.

text.KJV: For she is the brightness of the everlasting light, the unspotted mirror of the power of God, and the image of his goodness.
text.NABRE: For she is the reflection of eternal light, the spotless mirror of the power of God, the image of his goodness.
text.NRSV: For she is a reflection of eternal light, a spotless mirror of the working of God, and an image of his goodness.
text.DRB: For she is the brightness of eternal light, & the unspotted glass of God's majesty, and the image of his goodness.

text.KJV: And being but one, she can do all things: and remaining in herself, she maketh all things new: and in all ages entering into holy souls, she maketh them friends of God, and prophets.
text.NABRE: Although she is one, she can do all things, and she renews everything while herself perduring; Passing into holy souls from age to age, she produces friends of God and prophets.
text.NRSV: Although she is but one, she can do all things, and while remaining in herself, she renews all things; in every generation she passes into holy souls and makes them friends of God, and prophets;
text.DRB: And whereas she is one, she can do all things: and permanent in herself she reneweth all things, and by nations transporteth herself into holy souls, she maketh the friends of God, and Prophets.

text.KJV: For God loveth none but him that dwelleth with wisdom.
text.NABRE: For God loves nothing so much as the one who dwells with Wisdom.
text.NRSV: for God loves nothing so much as the person who lives with wisdom.
text.DRB: For God loveth none, but him that dwelleth with wisdom.

text.KJV: For she is more beautiful than the sun, and above all the order of stars: being compared with the light, she is found before it.
